title: The One Where Joey Tells Rachel

lines.speaker: Lisa Kudrow
lines.text: (voiceover) Previously on Friends
Central Perk, Joey is talking to Ross

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There's this woman, that I like.  A lot.  Well, it's complicated.  She's with this other guy.  For a long time.  And I could never do that to the guy, y'know?  'Ccause we're really good friends.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, uh, this guy, she used to go out with, is, uh... is he a good guy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, he's the best.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then talk to him! He might be fine with it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, it's worth finding out. I mean, if you really like her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I do! So much! I can't stop thinking about her! I can't sleep, I-

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, Joey, you know what? You have to go for it. How often does this happen to you, huh? You owe it to yourself. (Walks towards the door until...)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's Rachel.
[Fade to Black, then fade in again with Ross stopped at the doorway.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (closes the door) Did you um-I'm sorry, did you just say it's Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, you...you like Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes. I like Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (startled) Yeah, okay but look, buy uh-Hey-hey, y'know, y'know who else I like? You! And it-it doesn't get said enough. I like you Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But R-R-Rachel-Rachel?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but it's not a big deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's not a big deal? Oh, I'm sorry I just...um, I...what about all the stuff you-you just said? I mean how about, I like-you-you can't stop thinking about her. Like how you can't sleep?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm an actor, y'know? As-as a group, we tend to be over dramatic.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel who's carrying my baby? Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look no, I-I know it's bad, and I know it's wrong. Okay? But-but it's not like anything's ever gonna happen. Y'know? These-these are just feelings, they're gonna go away.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what? I-I gotta go. (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come on Ross! Hey Ross-Ross don't...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stops) I just-y'know-I-I just have one-Rachel?!
He exits and starts to walk away, passes a window, stops, and says "Rachel?!" again. Joey sighs and turns around to face Gunther.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Rachel?!!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Rachel and Chandler are on the couch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh! Okay, she's kicking!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! (Puts his hand on her belly.) She's growing inside you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! (Pulls his hand away.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow that was a big one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think that's the youngest girl ever to reject me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Oh hey you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey listen let me ask you, do you believe in soul mates?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yes I do. I do. I believe that there is one perfect person out there for everyone. And do you know how you find him? You stop looking for him. That's why I stopped looking for Russell Crowe. He'll find me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh, (To Chandler) and you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. I mean I believe that uh, certain people are more suited for each other and I believe in falling in love, but soul mates, I don't think they exist.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well last night, I met Monica's.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I had a date with this guy, and I swear to God, he is her other half.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on, don't be crazy. (To Rachel) You don't think there's someone out there better suited for Monica than me, do ya? (Rachel looks at him.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Well, what's he like?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well he's tall.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He has brown hair.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course, of course.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A tall guy with hair similar to mine, oh unknowable universe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He works with food!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh sure. Older?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Obviously, and he's British.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I was just gonna ask!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah? And, he's-he's so centered and mature and confident.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's so sad they never had a chance to meet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Luckily, the guy she settled for can't hear what you're talking about.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'm so sorry. Um, and maybe I'm wrong! I but-y'know I'm gonna go out with him again, I'll find out more.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You believe that this guy is destined for someone else and you're still gonna date him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he may not be my soul mate, but a girl's gotta eat.
Ross and Rachel's, Ross is opening the door to Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just talked to Joey, I just-I want to see how you were.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh y'know, I still-I can't believe it. Joey and Rachel I mean it's... It's like you and me going out, only weirder!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I know you're hurting, and-and I want to be supportive, but don't say that again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! What if, what if they get married? Then he'd be the stepfather of my child.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, I don't think that's something we need to worry about! First of all he's-he's never gonna tell her how he feels about her. And even if he did you have no idea how she'd react.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, because women never like Joey. Y'know, I think he's a virgin.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey...he's not even thinking about going after Rachel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He's not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! All he's thinking about is how you're taking this! I mean, listen it's totally freaking him out. He's talking about moving to Vermont.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He says he wants to leave the country. (Pause) He thinks you hate him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hate him? I... No, I don't hate him. (Pause) It's just it's Rachel, y'know?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey I can't even imagine how hard this must be for you. But, I don't want you to lose Joey over it. And right now he just needs to know that you're still his friend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay, I'll talk to him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. Now do it soon, he just asked me how to convert his dollars into Vermont money.
Central Perk, Monica and Chandler are on the couch as Phoebe enters with Monica's soul mate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, Chandler, Monica, this is Don.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Oh, hello. (Shakes Chandler's hand.) Hello. (Shakes Monica's hand.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (mouths to Chandler) Soul mate.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (mouths to Phoebe) What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (cough talks) Soul mate.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, what have you guys been doing?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Well, we just had a terrible lunch today at Reattica. What is with all the sun-dried tomatoes at that place?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! What is this, 1985?!

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: That's exactly what I said. Phoebe, isn't that strange.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's wrong with sun-dried tomatoes? (Everyone stares at him.) On a barbecue chicken pizza? (Still there's staring.) No?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So Don, what-what other restaurants do you like?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Well, Octavio, 27&7-Oh! And there's this great little place, Alessandro's.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! That's my restaurant, I'm the chef there.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: You're kidding me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Your food is fantastic! Wow, I really want to talk to you about your menu, once I get some coffees first. Um, anyone want any?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'd like a latte. Oh y'know what? If you're gonna talk about me, I'm gonna go with you.
They go over to the counter and Chandler moves closer to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing! I swear to God I didn't know you guys would be here! And the good news is you didn't believe in soul mates. So...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I believe in tall, handsome strangers who hit on my wife!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They're just talking, and y'know what? Just because I think they're soul mates doesn't mean anything's gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (returning) Phoebe, good work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Oh he's great, I love him. (Walks away and Chandler glares at Phoebe.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't worry, we'll find you someone else.
Joey's Apartment, there's a knock on the door and Joey answers it to Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I understand if you came by to hit me, I deserve it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I don't want to hit you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh what then? Kick me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (getting worried) Bite me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no I don't want to do anything to you. All right? I just want to tell you that I'm not mad at you and...and that I certainly do not hate you. I just, I just came here to say that. (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh now-Hey Ross-Ross! Do you wanna-wanna come in for a beer or something?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh...yeah sure. (Enters.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do uh, do you got any beer? All-all I got is this melon stuff that Rachel left. I don't...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pouring two glasses) Hey look Ross, you need to understand something okay? I uh...I am never gonna act on this Rachel thing, okay? I-I would never do anything to jeopardize my friendship with you. (He hands Ross a glass.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.
They clink glasses and take a drink. Ross likes it, Joey hates it. Then Ross sees Joey hating it, mimics he hates it as well.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Anyway, it uh...look it'll just...take me a while to get over her, that's all. I'm not even sure how to do that, I mean I've never been in love before so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! You're in love with her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I thought you knew that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow. Hey look, if it helps, I don't want to feel this way. Honest. I just keep thinking, "Ah, I'll get over this." Y'know? I just-It just keeps gettin' harder. I don't, I don't know what to do. Y'know? What do I do?
Pause

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think you need to tell her how you feel.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay that's the green stuff talkin'.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I'm serious. You-you need to find out where she is, because if she's not where you are, then you can start to move past this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But what if uh-and I'm not saying she will be-But...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: If she is where you are then uh...then my feeling weird about it shouldn't stand in the way.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Look if-if she's gonna end up with somebody else, the truth is she couldn't find a better guy. So...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So when do you think you're gonna talk to her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God I have to tell her! I haven't even thought about what I will say. What should I say?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm understanding, but let's not get carried away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sorry, you're right. What am I gonna say? (He takes another sip of the green stuff and recoils at the taste.) Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, tell me about it. (He stands up, turns his back to Joey, and enjoys another sip.)
Commercial Break
Central Perk, Phoebe and Chandler are watching Monica and Don talk.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: ...so I've been slowly phasing out the wine importing and focusing more on the cheese side of things.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Cheese you say? That's some pretty smelly work, huh Don?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Cheese, it's smelly. You must smell a lot of the time too.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Uh, not really. But when it comes to cheese, I'm one of the people who thinks the smellier the better.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too! Yeah, Chandler can't stand it. He won't even allow me to have blue cheese in the house.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: And you're still married to him? (They both laugh, and Chandler tries to but fails.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what I'd love to do? I would like to go to France and eat nothing but bread and cheese-Not even bread, just cheese. No, I want the bread. Yeah. Ah, and pastries... (Breathlessly) And pate. Oh, I'm really not high, it's just I used to be fat.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Well if you where ever enter the Loire valley let me know, I've got a great little villa you can stay at.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is it made of cheese?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: No. But God, a house made of cheese, wouldn't that be incredible?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'd move in tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (disgusted) Oh come on! (To Phoebe) Are you listening to this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do we do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know, they both want to live in a house of cheese! I don't know how you fight that.
Outside Ross and Rachel's, Joey walks up to knock on the door, but stops.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Okay, I can do this. I can tell her how I feel. Just uh, just stand up straight. (Does so.) Take a couple deep breaths. (Does so.) Look confident. (Does so as Rachel opens the door and startles him.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey? What's up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I uh, I just came by because I-I want to talk to you about something.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay what's up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here? In the hall? What are we animals?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well honey, I'm late for a meeting. So can you just make it quick?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay umm, I just came by to tell you that I...want to have dinner with you tonight. That's all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure! That sounds great! Just leave me a message and tell me where to meet you. Okay? (Walks away.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. That's good. Okay, that give you a couple hours to prepare what you're gonna say. Good. Yeah. (Someone comes out of his apartment and it startles Joey again.) Don't you people ever knock?!
Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are entering.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'd like to have Don and Phoebe over. Wouldn't that be nice?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure, why don't you set it up. I'll just be over here, browsing through the personals.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you okay? You've been acting weird all afternoon.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, fine. Fine. Not perfect!! But good enough.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Jeez! What is with you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry, did you say cheese?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe thinks you and Don are soul mates, and I don't believe in that kind of stuff. But then you two totally get along. So look, I won't stand in your way if you want to run off with Don and live in a house of cheese.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, you don't believe in soul mates?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. But I'm sure (mimics Don) 'tomatoes' does.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't believe in soul mates either.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. I don't think that you and I were destined to end up together. I think that we fell in love and work hard at our relationship. Some days we work really hard.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you...you don't want to live with Don in a cheese house?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I've had second thoughts about that. Do you realize how hard that would be to clean?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what? I am going to take you out to dinner tonight. I found this place that makes the greatest mozzarella sticks and jalepino poppers . (Monica doesn't look impressed.) No? Really? They taste so good.
A restaurant, Joey and Rachel are having dinner.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...and I know Chandler is kidding but it happens every time he touches my stomach. I mean I'm really worried the baby's not going to like him. (Joey is staring at the table.) Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Yeah! Sure! Uh, look at the uh, the reason...(Exhales slowly)-Is it hot in here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Not-not for me, but why don't you take off your sweater?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I would, but this is a nice place and my T-shirt has a picture of Calvin doing Hobbs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Really?! Can I see it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Sure. (They both half stand up, Joey pulls the neck of his sweater out, and Rachel looks down it to see his T-shirt.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Wow, I wouldn't think Hobbs would like that so much.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh... How long have we known each other?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, seven...e-e-eight, eight years. Wow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-huh, long time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But over the past few weeks...
A waiter runs over interrupting Joey.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Hah, sorry about the wait, but it is mega-jammed in here! We have a couple specials tonight...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually uh, could you give us a second?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Sure. Sure. (Turns away, then turns back) Second's up! (Joey glares at him.) Not...that kind of table. (He walks away.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So you were saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm not quite sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well you had asked me how long we had known each other, and I said, "Eight years." And the um, waiter came over and cut his tip in half, and umm...now here we are.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, here we are. Uhh... I... I think I'm...falling in love with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stunned) What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm falling in love with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking around) Who are you talking too? Oh, you're kidding! Oh, it's a joke! (Laughs.) It's funny. It's funny. I don't get it. (Joey doesn't say any thing and Rachel realizes it's not a joke.) Oh. (Pause) Okay. Umm... I-I...uh, wow. Are you uh... How did umm... When?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Does it really matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Wow. Wow. Wow, it is hot in here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay look Rach, I know this is a lot. You don't have to say anything. You-you uh, you take as much time as you need. (Long pause as Rachel says nothing.) Okay, you gotta say something!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, Joey I love you so much, but I...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But. (Hangs his head down.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah right. That's okay. That's fine. That's uh, pretty much what I was expecting. So uh, it's no big deal. All right? I think I'm gonna go. (Stands up.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Joey please! Please don't! Please don't leave like this! Now come on, you cannot do this to a pregnant woman! (Starts to cry.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't start doing that. You can't do that Rach, 'cause then you're gonna make me do that. (Starts to cry.) Oh, here we go! (Sits down next to her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can I? (Hug him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure!
They hug.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Joey honey I don't...I don't want to lose...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey, hey! You can't. Okay? Ever!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no Rach, please, don't be sorry. Okay? Don't be sorry. (They hug again.) Y'know I was only kidding you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, that was a real good one.
Closing Credits
A restaurant, Joey and Rachel are still hugging as a waiter walks by the table to talk to the annoying waiter from before who is watching.
Waiter No. 2: Is this your table?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Yeah.
Waiter No. 2: God, you're gonna be here all night!

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: I know! I haven't even read them the specials yet!
Waiter No. 2: What's the matter with them?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: I don't know. I think maybe one of them is dying. (Pause) I kinda hope it's the girl. (The other waiter is shocked.) The guy is really cute!
End
